title: Project Metadata, Resources, PyInstaller, and Platform Builds · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved maze trace contains one plausible coordinate, wall, graph, search, event-loop, focus, render, persistence, package, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in platform-native signed-off release artifact matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define package layout, metadata, Python requirement, dependencies, console or GUI entry point, and import-safe startup.
Load licensed package resources without assuming source checkout or current working directory layout.
Inspect PyInstaller analysis, tkinter hooks, Tcl/Tk libraries and data, hidden imports, warnings, and artifact contents.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Pyproject Entrypoint
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug puzzle team handles case 4114 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile package metadata, resources, Tcl and Tk assets, native artifacts, and clean-install launches; the prior good state must survive failure; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: maze-clean-install-smoke-q04vfu
# Competency: maze-clean-install-smoke.
# Case 8619: a corrupted-state recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_clean_install_smoke_q04vfu(target_platforms: int = 3, native_builds: int = 3, clean_launches: int = 3, missing_tk_assets: int = 0, artifact_digests: int = 3) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_q04vfu = "q04vfu-ufv40q-8619"
    assert evidence_variant_q04vfu
    evidence_axes_q04vfu = (
        "gridq04vfu",
        "topology8619",
        "searchufv40q",
        "eventq04vfu8619",
        "access8619q04vfu",
        "recoveryufv40q8619",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_q04vfu)) == 6
    if target_platforms < 1 or native_builds < 0:
        return False, native_builds, "platform-matrix"
    if native_builds != target_platforms or clean_launches != target_platforms:
        return False, clean_launches, "clean-install-gap"
    if missing_tk_assets > 0:
        return False, missing_tk_assets, "tk-resource-gap"
    if artifact_digests != target_platforms:
        return False, artifact_digests, "artifact-identity"
    assert clean_launches == target_platforms
    return True, clean_launches, "platform-native-release"
